topic: late-night food spots after Friday sets

turns.content: random question but what are good late night food spots in Toronto?? I need places that are open after 2am because I'm usually starving after my Friday sets and everywhere is CLOSED!!

Great question -- Toronto's late-night food scene has some solid options if you know where to look. Here are some spots that stay open past 2am:

1. **Owl of Minerva** (Bloor & Christie) -- Korean restaurant open 24 hours. Their bibimbap and soft tofu stew are perfect late-night comfort food.

2. **Ghazale** (multiple locations on Bloor) -- Middle Eastern spot, usually open until 3-4am on weekends. Shawarma wraps, falafel, really affordable too.

3. **King Slice** (multiple locations) -- giant pizza slices available until late. The Queen West location is usually packed with the after-bar crowd.

4. **Pho Tien Thanh** (Ossington) -- Vietnamese spot that's open late. A big bowl of pho at 3am hits different.

What part of the city are you usually in after your sets?

these are FIRE suggestions!! I'm usually downtown or west end area after sets. and honestly I want something that actually fills you up because I've been on my feet for 4-5 hours straight and a little snack isn't gonna cut it!!

For the downtown/west end area and a full meal, here are your best bets:

- **Vesta Lunch** (Dupont near Bathurst) -- classic diner, open until 4am on weekends. Huge portions -- burgers, all-day breakfast, poutine. It's been there since the 1950s and it's the real deal.

- **Sneaky Dee's** (College & Bathurst) -- kitchen stays open until 2:30am on weekends. Their nachos are legendary -- massive plate, fully loaded. They also do burritos and quesadillas.

- **7 West Cafe** (Charles Street) -- open 24 hours. Big menu, everything from pasta to steak to breakfast. A lot of industry people end up there after late shifts.

- **Commisso Brothers** (a few locations) -- Italian bakery/deli open 24/7. Fresh pizza, sandwiches, veal on a bun at any hour.

VESTA LUNCH!! I've walked past that place so many times and never went in!! 4am and they're still serving?? that's the energy I need lol. what about Caribbean food tho?? sometimes after a set I'm craving jerk chicken or oxtail BADLY

Caribbean spots in Toronto unfortunately tend to close earlier than other cuisines -- most are done by midnight. But here are a couple options:

- **Randy's Take-Out** (Eglinton West) -- this is a Jamaican institution in Toronto. They sometimes stay open until 2-3am on Friday and Saturday nights, but it's worth calling ahead to confirm. Their jerk chicken and oxtail are the real thing.

- **Albert's Real Jamaican Food** (multiple locations) -- the Bathurst location sometimes does late hours on weekends.

- **Island Foods** (Eglinton area) -- another legit spot, though hours vary.

Honestly, Little Jamaica on Eglinton West is your best bet for Caribbean late-night, but getting up there from downtown after 2am adds travel time. For guaranteed late-night Caribbean, you might have to cook a big batch of something on Thursday night and just heat it up when you get home.

lol meal prep on Thursday for my Friday night food?? that's actually not a bad idea still!! I just don't want to be cooking at 3am when I finally get home because I'm WRECKED after a set. I just want to eat and crash

Exactly -- cook once, eat when you need it. Make a big pot of curry goat or stew chicken on Thursday, portion it out, and when you get home Friday night just microwave and go. Rice takes like 15 minutes or you can make that ahead too.

Alternatively, some people swear by those insulated food containers -- cook your meal before the set, pack it in a thermal container, and it's still warm when you're done at 2am. Saves you from relying on what's open.

But for nights when you want to eat out and decompress after a set, I'd say Vesta Lunch and 7 West Cafe are your most reliable downtown options. They're always open and the portions will actually fill you up.

the thermal container idea is GENIUS!! ok I think my plan is: meal prep on Thursdays, thermal container for sets, and Vesta Lunch for when I want to actually sit down somewhere and eat. sorted!! you just solved a problem I've had for MONTHS lol thank you!!

Ha, glad I could help! That's a solid three-part system. And honestly, having a regular post-set food routine takes one more decision off your plate when you're exhausted. Enjoy Vesta Lunch when you try it -- get the poutine at least once. It's a Toronto late-night rite of passage.
